data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise as Production Hits Record
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup


U.S. crude oil inventories increased more than expected last week as U.S. production hit a record high and exports fell, according to data released Thursday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Commercial crude oil stocks excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 6.4 million barrels, to 427.6 million barrels, in the week ended Nov. 7, and were about 4% below the five-year average for the time of year, the EIA said. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey had expected a 400,000 barrel crude stock build.

U.S. crude oil production rose by 211,000 barrels a day, to just under 13.9 million barrels a day, the highest on record, according to EIA estimates. Crude imports were down by 703,000 barrels a day, at 5.2 million barrels a day, while exports fell by 1.6 million barrels a day, to 2.8 million barrels a day.

Oil held in the SPR increased by 798,000 barrels, to 410.4 million barrels. Stocks at Cushing, Okla., the Nymex delivery hub, were down by 346,000 barrels, at 22.5 million barrels.

Refinery capacity use rose by 3.4 percentage points, to 89.4%, against expectations in the Journal survey for a 0.6 perentage-point increase. Crude input to refineries was up by 717,000 barrels a day, at 16 million barrels a day.

Gasoline inventories fell by 945,000 barrels, to 205.1 million barrels, and were 4% below the five-year average, the EIA said. Gasoline demand averaged 9 million barrels a day, up by 145,000 barrels a day from the week before. Gasoline stocks were seen falling by 2 million barrels.

Distillate fuel stocks fell by 637,000 barrels, to 110.9 million barrels, and were 8% below the five-year average. Distillate inventories were forecast to have declined by 1.5 million barrels. 


 
Change in U.S. oil inventories for the week ended Nov. 7: 
 
                   Crude       Gasoline      Distillates        Refinery Use 
EIA data:           6.4          -0.9           -0.6                 3.4 
Forecast:           0.4          -2.0           -1.5                 0.6

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.


Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1249ET

data.title: Alstom raises FY organic sales outlook
IDEX Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.71 per Share, Payable Jan. 30, 2026, to Holders of Record Jan. 16, 2026
Monte dei Paschi completes EUR 500 mln green bond issue
NYMEX Overview : Oil Rebounds After Wednesday's Selloff -- OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil contracts were trading higher around midday Thursday as the petroleum market regained its footing after the previous session's steep losses driven by renewed oversupply fears.

At 12:05 p.m. ET, the more-active January NYMEX West Texas Intermediate crude futures were up by 30cts to about $58.80/bbl, and December WTI was 35cts higher to $58.85/bbl.

London-based January ICE Brent prices were up by 40cts to around $63.15/bbl and February Brent was 35cts higher to $62.80/bbl.

Both WTI and Brent finished almost $3 lower on Wednesday after OPEC said in its latest monthly forecast that global supply could catch up to demand by 2026.

Earlier Thursday, the Paris-based International Energy Agency in its monthly report raised its forecasts for global oil supply and demand for this year and 2026, which would result in even larger surpluses for both years.

Refined product futures were mixed. January NYMEX RBOB was up by 2.1cts to $1.909/gal and December RBOB was 2.6cts higher to $1.9815/gal. January ULSD, however, was down 1.3cts to $2.4245/gal and December ULSD was 1.4cts lower to $2.468/gal.

Petroleum futures did not show dramatic changes immediately following the release of the weekly data by the Energy Information Administration. The latest EIA data showed U.S. commercial crude oil stocks increased 6.4 million bbl for the week ended Friday, while gasoline and distillate inventory were both down by less than 1 million bbl.

In the spot market, U.S. Gulf Coast CBOB gasoline rose nearly 5cts ahead of a scheduling deadline later Thursday. Other cash products mostly mirrored changes in NYMEX futures.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 


   --Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly,   mkelly@opisnet.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1246ET

data.description:
Eureka Forbes Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 7,720.79 million compared to INR 6,731.4 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 7,780.56 million compared to INR 6,765.33 million a year ago. Net income was INR 628.96 million compared to INR 477.12 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 3.23 compared to INR 2.47 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 3.23 compared to INR 2.46 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 13,799.49 million compared to INR 12,265.19 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 13,933.66 million compared to INR 12,322.52 million a year ago. Net income was INR 1,015.75 million compared to INR 798.59 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 5.23 compared to INR 4.13 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 5.23 compared to INR 4.12 a year ago.

Pfizer Completes Acquisition of Metsera
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan


Pfizer closed its acquisition of weight-loss drug startup Metsera in a deal that could be valued at more than $10 billion.

Under the deal, Pfizer paid $65.60 a share in cash up front, and could additionally pay a contingent value right of up to $20.65 a share tied to specific clinical and regulatory milestones, the pharmaceutical company said Thursday.

Metsera is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer, and its shares will stop trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market after market close, Pfizer said.

The deal marks an investment in the future of medicine, according to Pfizer.

"By acquiring Metsera, we are directing our resources toward one of the most impactful and high-growth therapeutic areas and positioning ourselves to define it," Pfizer Chief Executive Albert Bourla said.

The deal follows a heated bidding war with rival Novo Nordisk. In September, Pfizer had agreed to buy Metsera for up to $7.3 billion, which Novo Nordisk topped with an unsolicited bid valued at up to $9 billion.

After Pfizer reached its deal with Metsera last week, Novo Nordisk said it wouldn't increase its offer because of its commitment to financial discipline and shareholder value. The company said it will keep assessing opportunities for business development and acquisition.

Metsera, meanwhile, said it decided to take Pfizer's offer in part because of a call from the Federal Trade Commission warning about potential risks to proceeding with a Novo Nordisk deal.


Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1239ET

data.title: Honda, Nissan in Talks to Develop Powertrains in US
Toronto Stocks Pull Back; AtkinsRealis Gains on Nuclear-Segment Outlook
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


Toronto stocks were in retreat as most sectors pulled back.

Tech, utilities and health-tech stocks led the declines. Only the consumer-services, energy and consumer-discretionary sectors were ahead.

Canada's S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 1%, to 30515.62, midday Thursday, and the blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 declined 0.9%, to 1798.43.

AtkinsRealis Group shares rose 4.6%, to 92.82 Canadian dollars (US$66.26), after the engineering firm raised its outlook for its nuclear division even as it lowered overall organic revenue growth targets. The company also logged a increase in third-quarter revenue and profit.


Other market movers:

Northland Power's shares pulled back sharply, falling 24%, to C$19.13, after the power producer scaled back its dividend to strengthen its ability to fund its pipeline of development projects.

Ballard Power Systems reported a narrower loss in the third quarter thanks to its heavy-duty mobility segment driving revenue higher. Shares were down 6.7%, to C$4.47.

Brookfield saw strong earnings growth in the third quarter as the investment firm benefited from sustained growth across its wealth-solutions platform and strong asset-management fund raising. The stock slipped 7.5%, to C$60.77.

Hydro One logged stronger earnings in the third quarter as it approved 2025 transmission and distribution rates buoyed revenue. Shares rose 1.2%, to C$53.27.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1236ET

data.title: Alstom hikes 2025 revenue outlook after first-half sales beat
data.description:
(Reuters) -French train maker Alstom on Thursday lifted its 2025 organic sales outlook after posting half-year revenue above expectations, driven by strong demand for its high-speed trains.

Alstom, which makes trains and signalling systems for urban and regional rail networks, now anticipates organic sales will rise above 5%, compared with its previous guidance of 3% to 5%.

"Strong sales growth across all product lines demonstrates our ability to accelerate backlog delivery and achieve full-year growth above our initial expectations." said chief executive Henri Poupart-Lafarge.

Revenue rose 8% on a organic basis to 9.06 billion euros ($10.57 billion) in the first half of the year. Analysts had expected 8.97 billion euros, according to a company compiled consensus.

The increase was notably driven by a "strong performance" in Europe, where group revenue rose 8.5% year-on-year in the first half of the year.

Alstom has signed several major contracts this quarter - including a 6.9 billion zloty ($1.9 billion) contract  with Poland's national long-distance rail operator PKP Intercity and a 1.4 billion euro Alstom high speed train order from French rail operator SNCF Voyageurs for its Eurostar routes.

The group confirmed the rest of its guidance for the year.

($1 = 0.8575 euros)

(Reporting by Alban Kacher; Editing by Matt Scuffham)

data.title: Coca Cola Insider Sold Shares Worth $2,864,915, According to a Recent SEC Filing
Enel Lifts Guidance After International Operations Boost Earnings
data.description:
By Mauro Orru


Enel raised its annual guidance for a key profitability metric after posting higher earnings for the first nine months, aided by its operations in Spain and Colombia.

The Rome-based energy company said net ordinary income this year should come in slightly above the high end of a range between 6.7 billion and 6.9 billion euros ($7.77 billion-$8 billion).

Meanwhile, ordinary earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--another closely watched metric of operating profitability--are still expected to range between 22.9 billion and 23.1 billion euros.

The upgrade comes after Enel said net ordinary income for the first nine months grew 4.5% on year to 5.70 billion euros. Reported net profit came in at 5.24 billion euros compared with 5.25 billion euros a year earlier.

Ebitda fell slightly to 16.87 billion euros from 16.92 billion euros, but ordinary Ebitda rose 0.9% to 17.26 billion euros. Enel said its operations in Spain and Colombia had more-than offset a reduction of margins in Italy.

Revenue increased to 59.70 billion euros from 57.63 billion euros a year earlier, mainly thanks to higher commodity sales on the wholesale market amid rising average prices.

Enel said it approved an interim dividend for 2025 of 0.23 euros a share to be paid starting from Jan. 21.


Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 1235ET

Enel grows in the first nine months and pays an interim dividend; guidance confirmed
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Enel Spa announced on Thursday that it had recorded revenues of EUR 59.7 billion in the first nine months, up 3.6% from EUR 57.6 billion in the same period of 2024, thanks mainly to higher commodity sales on the wholesale market in a context of rising prices.

Ordinary EBITDA stood at EUR17.26 billion, up 0.9%, supported by positive contributions from Spain and Colombia, which offset lower margins in Italy due to lower average prices and reduced water availability.

The group's ordinary net profit rose to EUR 5.70 billion, or 4.5% compared to EUR 5.46 billion in the first nine months of 2024, thanks to solid operational management and lower financial expenses resulting from lower debt and a lower average cost of debt.

EBIT amounted to EUR10.92 billion, down 14% due to higher depreciation and amortization related to renewable plants in the US and Chile.

Net financial debt amounted to EUR 57.54 billion, up 3.2% compared to the end of 2024, following investments of EUR 6.5 billion, dividend payments of EUR 5.9 billion and the purchase of treasury shares, only partially offset by positive cash flows and the favorable effect of exchange rates.

Total investments amounted to EUR 6.84 billion, down 10% compared to the previous nine months.

The group confirmed its full-year guidance, with ordinary EBITDA expected to be between EUR22.9 billion and EUR23.1 billion and ordinary net profit slightly above the upper end of the range of EUR6.7-6.9 billion.

The board of directors therefore approved an interim dividend for 2025 of EUR0.23 per share, up 7% on the previous year, with payment on January 21, 2026, ex-dividend date on January 19, and record date on January 20.

The dividend policy, consistent with the 2025-2027 strategic plan, provides for a minimum of EUR 0.46 per share and a possible increase up to a payout of 70% of the group's ordinary net profit.

Enel also reiterated the three pillars of the 2025-2027 plan - profitability and resilience, operational efficiency, and financial and environmental sustainability - and confirmed investments of approximately EUR 43 billion, of which EUR 26 billion will be allocated to networks and EUR 12 billion to renewables.

Enel shares closed Thursday up 0.2% at EUR8.96 per share.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com
